blocks.content: They walked. It was late enough that Millhaven was quiet — the streetlights orange-white, no cars.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Eli's going to be insufferable about that.
They walked another half block. The neighborhood started to look familiar — her block, almost.
She stopped walking. He stopped too. She turned to look at him under the streetlight, and he could see her clearly — the pin in her hair, the dress.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I need you to know something.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I don't want to go to August without saying this. Because I think if I wait until August everything gets harder and bigger and I'll lose my nerve.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I've been in love with you since I was twelve years old and I don't know what to do about August and I don't have an answer for any of it. But I needed you to know that. Before we go.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Since we were twelve. Same. I just — it's always been you. I need you to know that too.
He reached out. She let him take her hand. They stood on the sidewalk under the streetlight holding hands.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I still don't know what to do about August.
She laughed — a real one, small and warm — and leaned her head against his shoulder, and they stood there like that for a long moment before she lifted her head and looked at him. Close.
